title: Reptilian flier

'''Reptilian fliers''' (or '''flyers'''), also known as '''saurian fliers''', and '''saurians''', were a large [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Diet/Legends|carnivorous]], winged [[Reptavian/Legends|reptavians]] native to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]]. [[Beastmaster/Legends|Beast masters]] were able to domesticate saurians as personal companions or mountable beasts of burden. They could also be [[Death/Legends|killed]] and harvested for their [[Leather/Legends|leathery]] hides, [[Skeleton/Legends|bones]] and meat. Deadly and ferocious, a number of saurians attacked [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] when, during [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]], [[Gender/Legends|he]] embarked on a [[Mission to Dathomir (Post–Naboo crisis)|mission to Dathomir]]. Vos, after being freed from his [[Ejector seat/Legends|ejector seat]], used his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] to defeat the aggressive beasts. During the [[Time/Legends|time]] following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], a [[Dathomirian/Legends|Dathomirian]] [[Witches of Dathomir|witch]] named [[Rubina]] offered at least one [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] a decorative [[Reptilian Flyer Model|model of a reptilian flier]] as a gift.

==Biology and appearance==
The [[Diet/Legends|carnivorous]]<ref name="FactFile" /> [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Reptavian/Legends|reptavians]] known as [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] fliers,<ref name="AED" /> but also as saurian fliers or saurians, were a type of large [[Bird/Legends|bird]] native to [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]],<ref name="FactFile" /> an obscure [[Planet/Legends|world]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="GATORW">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref> Flocks of these dangerous creatures filled the skies,<ref name="FactFile" /> and roamed the [[Forest/Legends|forests]] and plains of the remote world. The reptilian, lizardlike [[vertebrate]]s had [[Leather/Legends|leathery]] hides, the [[Color/Legends|color]] of which typically included subdued palettes of grey, red<ref name="AED" /> or blue in the wild.<ref name="FactFile" /> However, when domesticated, their hides could come in a variety of different hues, including shades of orange and green.<ref name="AED" /> Distinguishing features of the [[Limb/Legends|bipedal]] creature included large, [[Bat/Legends|batlike]] wings, long tails, and horned heads featuring sharp [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]], and beady red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Reptilian fliers attacked both aerial and ground targets with [[Death/Legends|deadly]] speed and incredible ferocity. The four grasping claws on their feet allowed the winged creatures to grab their prey and tear them to pieces with their powerful jaws. Tougher victims were borne aloft by the creatures and dropped to their death, only to be subsequently consumed by the violent beasts, who left behind only a few larger [[Skeleton/Legends|bones]] and a spattering of remains.<ref name="FactFile" /> When vocalizing, they would emit a screech or cawing noise.<ref name="AED" />

==History==
Reptilian fliers could be killed and harvested for their resources, including their leathery hide, bones and meat.<ref name="AED" /> A number of them attacked [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] in [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]] when [[Gender/Legends|he]] first [[Mission to Dathomir (Post–Naboo crisis)|arrived]] on Dathomir in an [[Ejector seat/Legends|ejector seat]]. The beasts tore him free of the seat's restraints, allowing him access to his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], which he used to defeat them.<ref name="Infinity">[[Star Wars (1998) 23|''Star Wars'' (1998) 23]]</ref> Reptilian fliers could also be domesticated by [[Beastmaster/Legends|beast masters]] as personal companions or as a form of transport unto themselves as mountable beasts of burden.<ref name="AED" />
{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars Galaxies}}
By the [[Time/Legends|time]] following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]<ref name="AED" /> in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> a [[Dathomirian/Legends|Dathomirian]] [[Witches of Dathomir|witch]] by the name of [[Rubina]] offered at least one [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] a decorative [[Reptilian Flyer Model|model of a reptilian flier]] for their success in eliminating [[mutant]] [[Rancor/Legends|rancors]] and members of the rival [[Spiderclan/Legends|Spider Clan]].<ref name="AED" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:ReptilianFlier.jpg|200px|thumb|A mature reptilian flier]]
These reptavian creatures first appeared in the [[2000]] ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' comic, [[Star Wars (1998) 23|''Star Wars'' (1998) 23]], however they were not specifically named.<ref name="Infinity" /> The species was first identified as [[W:c:swg:Reptilian Flier|reptilian fliers]] (or flyers), and included as [[W:c:swg:Creatures & enemies|creatures]] in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']], a [[Roleplaying game|massively multiplayer online role-playing game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]], prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="AED" /><ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> In addition to the typical reptilian flier, the game included others types of named fliers, such as ancient reptilian fliers, enraged reptilian fliers, mature reptilian fliers, and young reptilian fliers, all of which could be found on [[W:c:swg:Dathomir|Dathomir]]. While the game did not explicitly refer to the creatures as reptavians, it did refer to them as "reptilian," and their [[W:c:swg:Creature resources|resources]] (such as their [[W:c:swg:Avian bones|bones]] and [[W:c:swg:Avian meat|meat]]) were referred to as "avian." Therefore, their distinction as reptavians can be inferred.<ref name="AED" />

The creature is also listed as a reptilian flier in several reference guides for [[W:c:swg:Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies'']], including the ''[[Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]'' (2003),<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> the ''[[Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided Quick Reference Guide]]'' (2003),<ref name="QuickRef">''[[Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided Quick Reference Guide]]''</ref> the ''[[Star Wars Galaxies: Jump to Lightspeed: Prima Official Game Guide]]'' ([[2004]]),<ref name="JTL">[[Star Wars Galaxies: Jump to Lightspeed: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars Galaxies: Jump to Lightspeed'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref> the ''[[Star Wars Galaxies: The Total Experience: Prima Official Game Guide]]'' (2005),<ref name="Total">[[Star Wars Galaxies: The Total Experience: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars Galaxies: The Total Experience'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref> and the ''[[Star Wars Galaxies: The Complete Guide: Prima Official Game Guide]]'' (2005).<ref name="Complete">[[Star Wars Galaxies: The Complete Guide: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars Galaxies: The Complete Guide'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref> However, ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 139|139]] (2004) was the first to identify the creature with the slightly different name of saurian flier. The magazine also collated additional information on the overall behavior and characteristics of the creature.<ref name="FactFile" />

With the release of ''Star Wars Galaxies'', reptilian fliers could be [[W:c:swg:Tame (Ability)|domesticated]] as [[W:c:swg:Pets|pets]] by players who pursued the [[W:c:swg:Creature Handler|creature handler]] [[W:c:swg:Professions (pre-NGE)|profession]].<ref name="AED" /> Following the release of [[W:c:swg:Publish 25 Notes for November 15 2005|Publish 25's]] [[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]] on [[November 15]], [[2005]], creature handlers were removed.<ref name="AED" /><ref name="NGE">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64886&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2005}}</ref> However, with the release of [[W:c:swg:Chapter 6 Notes for May 22 2007|Chapter 6: Masters of the Wild]] on [[May 22]], [[2007]], [[W:c:swg:Beast Master|beast masters]] were added as an [[W:c:swg:Expertise|expertise]] path,<ref name="Chapter6">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=66959&resource=publish#chapter6|text=Chapter 6: Masters of the Wild}}</ref> allowing players to incubate domesticated creatures.<ref name="AED" /> With the release of [[W:c:swg:Game Update 17|Game Update 17]] on [[June 24]], [[2010]], reptilian fliers could be successfully trained as [[W:c:swg:Mount|mounts]].<ref name="GU17">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=66962&resource=publish#gu170|text=''Star Wars'' Galaxies: Game Update 17}}</ref> When the [[W:c:swg:Witches of Dathomir|Witches of Dathomir]] [[W:c:swg:Witches of Dathomir Theme Park|theme park]] was revamped as part of [[W:c:swg:Game Update 18.2|Game Update 18.2]] on [[November 4]], [[2010]],<ref name="GU18">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=66962&resource=publish#gu182|text=''Star Wars'' Galaxies: Witches of Dathomir Prologue - Game Update 18.2}}</ref> and [[W:c:swg:Game Update 19|Game Update 19]] on [[December 14]], 2010,<ref name="GU19">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=66962&resource=publish#gu190|text=''Star Wars'' Galaxies: Game Update 19}}</ref> a non-player character named [[W:c:swg:Rubina|Rubina]] would offer players a [[W:c:swg:Reptilian Flyer Model|Reptilian Flyer Model]] as a reward option for completing [[W:c:swg:Quest|quests]] requiring the [[W:c:swg:Eliminate the Mutant Rancors|elimination of mutant rancors]] or [[W:c:swg:Eliminate members of the Spider Clan|members of the Spider Clan]].<ref name="AED" /> The game's directory tree files include a description of the reptilian flier that is not definitively known to have appeared on the live [[W:c:swg:Server|servers]]. The description likens the flier to a "big flying lizard" which would swoop down from the [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] of Dathomir to carry off small children and other "tasty snacks" from the forest below.<ref name="TRE">{{GalaxiesAED}} directory tree files and source data</ref>
